title: Pipeline Dependencies: Stall and Forwarding
summary: Covers the three types of pipeline dependencies (structural, data, control), their causes and effects on throughput, and the solutions: stall and forwarding.
content_points: Structural: two instructions need the same resource simultaneously (e.g., memory, ALU).
Data: instruction 2 needs a result not yet computed by instruction 1 (RAW, WAR, WAW).
Control: jump instructions change PC late; following instructions fetched incorrectly; pipeline may be flushed.
~1 in 7 instructions is a jump – significant performance impact.
Stall: insert bubble (NOP) until dependency resolves. Simple but slow.
Forwarding: route result directly from ALU output to dependent instruction input; reduces stalls.

examples.name: Data dependency
examples.description: 'mov eax,7; sub eax,3' – sub must wait for mov to write eax.

examples.name: Forwarding
examples.description: 'add byte ptr [eax],5; sub ecx,[eax]' – sub takes addition result directly from ALU.

learning_objectives: Identify structural, data, and control pipeline dependencies
Explain the impact of stalls on throughput
Describe forwarding and its hardware requirements
